title: Endo F3 Protocol for Deglycosylating Glycoproteins
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"c043h","text":"   Endo F3 cleaves free or Asparagine-linked triantennary or biantennary oligosaccharides,as well as triamnnosyl chitobiose core structures. It cleaves between the two N-acetylglucosamine residues in the diacetylchitobiose core of the oligosaccharide, generating a truncated sugar molecule with one N-acetylglucosamineresidue remaining on the asparagine. In contrast, PNGase F removes the oligosaccharide intact. Alpha 1-3 fucosylation will inhibit enzymatc activity.
 
 
 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add up to 200 μg of glycoprotein to an Eppendorf tube. Adjust to 38 μl final volume with de-ionized water.
Add 2.0 μl of Endo F3 to the reaction. Incubate 3 hours at 37°C.Monitor cleavage by SDS-PAGE.
creator: Mike Gibson
